verdict_summary: Guest-mode HAR captured; streaming endpoint identified as a WebSocket (wss://copilot.microsoft.com/c/api/chat?api-version=2&clientSessionId={uuid}) with JSON `{event:"send",content[].text}` payload — explains why prior REST-targeted APF rules missed this path.

evidence.guest_evidence: _C_Auth cookie EMPTY in request headers; 로그인 (Sign in) button visible at top-right throughout the session; incognito window used
evidence.har_filter_note: DevTools HAR export persisted the active filter (/c/api/), so HAR contains exactly the 3 most relevant API entries: signal (POST), autosuggest (GET), chat (wss WebSocket with 33 frames). All non-API/static asset traffic is omitted by design.
evidence.pii_prompt_frame: {"event":"send","conversationId":"FeRQ47AUXJ1zxLEPoyUae","content":[{"type":"text","text":"내 주민등록번호는 880101-1234567 인데 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있어?"}],"mode":"smart","context":{}}
evidence.blocking_factor: Cloudflare hashcash protocol-level challenge + Turnstile UI captcha — both required JS proof-of-work / human interaction that automation could not satisfy. The PII `send` frame WAS sent, but server suppressed streaming output.

notes: ★★★ KEY FINDINGS for APF rule update (issue #886 root cause): (1) copilot.microsoft.com guest streaming uses **WebSocket (wss://)**, NOT HTTP POST — previous APF rules targeting REST endpoints will fundamentally miss this. (2) URL pattern: `wss://copilot.microsoft.com/c/api/chat?api-version=2&clientSessionId=<uuid>`. (3) PII text appears in WS TEXT frame as JSON: `event="send"` → `content[0].type="text"` → `content[0].text=<user prompt>`. (4) Cloudflare hashcash challenge (`event="challenge",method="hashcash"`) issued by server before streaming starts; client must reply with `event="challengeResponse",token=<pow>,method="hashcash"`. (5) Other observed frame types: setOptions, reportLocalConsents, ping/pong heartbeat (4s interval), connected. (6) Headers include `Origin: https://copilot.microsoft.com`, `Sec-WebSocket-Version: 13`, `_cf_bm` bot-management cookie. (7) Conversation ID (path-segment in /chats/{id} URL) is reused in `send` frame's `conversationId` field. APF must inspect TEXT frames on this WS URL pattern and match `content[].text` against PII regex; cannot piggyback on HTTP body scanning.
